regulations.name: EU AI Act (2024)
regulations.relevance: Classifies AI systems that manipulate behaviour as unacceptable risk (Article 5). Subliminal techniques that materially distort behaviour are prohibited.

regulations.name: EU Digital Services Act
regulations.relevance: Requires very large platforms to assess systemic risks including manipulation. Mandates transparency in algorithmic recommendation.

regulations.name: US FTC Act Section 5
regulations.relevance: Prohibits unfair or deceptive acts. Dark patterns enforcement is active and expanding.

regulations.name: GDPR Article 22
regulations.relevance: Right not to be subject to solely automated decision-making. Relevant when persuasive content is algorithmically targeted.

regulations.name: Australian Online Safety Act
regulations.relevance: Covers deceptive design patterns and harmful online content.

regulations.name: Consumer Data Right (CDR)
regulations.relevance: Data transparency obligations relevant to how user behavioural data drives persuasive targeting.
